sourceHighlights.title: Homebound reason
sourceHighlights.summary: The patient is considered homebound because she is experiencing significant functional limitations following a left total knee arthroplasty. Prior to surgery, she was unable to ambulate safely due to severe left knee osteoarthritis, with pain interfering with outside mobility, preventing stair climbing, and limiting ambulation to less than 200 meters. Post-operatively, the patient requires an assistive device and the assistance of another person to leave home. Leaving home requires a considerable and taxing effort.

sourceHighlights.title: Medical necessity
sourceHighlights.summary: Skilled Nursing treatment may need to be continued after discharge in a rehabilitation facility or skilled nursing facility.

draftNarratives.label: Homebound Reason
draftNarratives.draft: The patient is considered homebound because she is experiencing significant functional limitations following a left total knee arthroplasty. Prior to surgery, she was unable to ambulate safely due to severe left knee osteoarthritis, with pain interfering with outside mobility, preventing stair climbing, and limiting ambulation to less than 200 meters. Post-operatively, the patient requires an assistive device and the assistance of another person to leave home. Leaving home requires a considerable and taxing effort.

draftNarratives.label: Medical Necessity
draftNarratives.draft: Skilled Nursing treatment may need to be continued after discharge in a rehabilitation facility or skilled nursing facility.

draftNarratives.label: Patient Summary / Clinical Narrative
draftNarratives.draft: The patient is a 75-year-old female who presents for a pre-operative visit for a left total knee arthroplasty scheduled for 3/11/2026. The patient has insidious onset of knee pain, cannot take stairs due to pain, and cannot walk more than 200 meters at a time due to knee pain. She has attempted nonsteroidal anti-inflammatories and a therapeutic exercise program without success. The patient has a history of abdominal surgery, gallbladder surgery, and tubal ligation. She has a history of hypertension, hyperlipidemia, hypothyroidism, gastro-esophageal reflux disease, asthma, depression, and diabetes. The patient has a BMI of 34.28 kg/m² and a pain level of 8/10.

draftNarratives.label: Skilled Interventions
draftNarratives.draft: Physical Therapy Occupational Therapy physical therapy.

draftNarratives.label: Plan For Next Visit
draftNarratives.draft: Physical Therapy Occupational Therapy physical therapy.

draftNarratives.label: Care Plan Problems / Goals / Interventions
draftNarratives.draft: Problems: Arthritis (osteo, primary), Knee - LT (M17.12). Goals: To manage pain and improve mobility post-operatively. Interventions: Physical Therapy Protocol, Diet Education, Knee X-Ray, 3 Views (Taken and Read) - - LT, PT Eval & Treat: Knee Physical Therapy Protocol.

draftNarratives.label: Patient / Caregiver Goals
draftNarratives.draft: The patient and caregiver goals include managing pain, improving mobility, and adhering to the post-operative rehabilitation protocol.
